purpose: Machine-readable requirements for current commercial Console objects.
machineBoundary: Current commercial business objects, route ownership, required route/action capabilities, and evidence requirements.

lifecycle.removalCondition: Replace only with a newer current contract for the same boundary.

productTruth: OPL Console / OPL Cloud Console commercial launch slice
currentSlice: Business-chain closure for wallet, stable Workspace URL subjects, package-level compute pools, account-owned compute allocations, independent storage volumes, runtime attachments, resource usage ledger, support, and admin operations.
principles: Active business object contract contains only current commercial objects.
Workspace is the stable commercial object: stable URL, persistent StorageVolume, and current runtime pointer.
ComputePool is the package-level TKE node pool; ComputeAllocation is one replaceable account-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node in that pool.
Fabric-managed ComputePools must not auto-scale or auto-repair user nodes; every account CVM must be created, owned, billed, and destroyed as a Console ComputeAllocation.
StorageVolume can exist without compute; StorageAttachment mounts one volume to one current ComputeAllocation runtime.
Destroying compute suspends the Workspace URL and retains storage; rebuilding compute reuses the same Workspace URL and StorageVolume.
Destroying storage makes the Workspace unrecoverable because the file body is gone.
Business objects are protected by route ids, action ids, API clients, permission boundaries, and tests.
Lab Owner sees business outcomes; Admin sees operator evidence and commercial operations.
Future approval, policy, raw provider diagnostics, snapshot/recovery, and retired runtime lifecycle objects live outside the active contract until implemented.
repoBoundaryRules: Console owns UI, auth, route contracts, and read-model orchestration.
Fabric owns runtime, storage, connector, and agent resource execution boundaries.
Ledger owns evidence, audit, reconciliation, and review policy boundaries.

objectKinds.boundary: Stable URL/token subject backed by one persistent StorageVolume and a current replaceable ComputeAllocation/StorageAttachment runtime pointer.
Package-level TKE node pool for one compute specification. It is a placement pool, not an account resource identity.
Account-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node allocation inside a package ComputePool; nodePoolId is the placement pool, not the compute resource identity.
Account-owned storage that can exist without compute and can attach to one active ComputeAllocation at a time.
Mounts one StorageVolume to one ComputeAllocation runtime.
Account-level map of compute allocations, storage volumes, storage attachments, and Workspace URL entries.
Safe read-only summary of real production E2E verification records derived from existing ledger and operation evidence.
